hard.next_step: 6/3/26 MQ reaching out to Arvind to schedule post-merger discovery; position a focused ~2-month-out discussion on supplier onboarding and manual-analytics ROI (not a generic full-suite demo) and pre-send onboarding + spend-analytics materials, asking Arvind for 1-2 data points to tailor an ROI view.
hard.ais_why: TEGNA already runs AI apps for invoicing/AP as part of the post-merger transformation with Nexstar; Arvind wants quantified ROI on manual analytics rather than a generic full-suite demo; AI-driven AP transformation is core to the merger thesis, keeping the AI conversation warm even without a recent call.
hard.competitor: Do-nothing / status quo (manual analytics, Oracle Fusion punch-out, iClient CLM); post-merger imposition of Nexstar's S2P stack is the unknown

north_star_verdict.headline: Arvind's buyer-requested two-month circle-back window (early June) has now passed, and Marc's 3 June re-engagement email is unanswered a month later, so the one live thread has gone quiet while the TEGNA-Nexstar merger freeze holds; still a valid deal, but re-engagement is stalled and needs an owner-led restart.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 9 Apr 2026, Qualified since 23 Apr 2026 (74 days in stage as of 6 Jul). Close 16 Mar 2027 is 253 days out. Only buyer two-way exchange is the 7 Apr 2026 intro call (90 days ago); Arvind asked to 'circle back in two months' (early June) due to merger legal limbo. Rep sent re-engagement emails 9 Apr and 3 Jun; the 3 Jun ask (33 days ago) drew no buyer reply. The pause was buyer-imposed and time-bounded, but the window has closed with no buyer response, so momentum has thinned. No date slip; the far close date absorbs the freeze, so this is Slowing (stalled re-engagement), not Off Track.
evidence: Last genuine buyer exchange was the 7 Apr 2026 intro call; Arvind Swamy ended it with 'Yeah. Let's circle back in two months' because of the TEGNA-Nexstar merger legal limbo ('we're kind of in a holding period where we can't sign anything, we can't move anything').

Marc Quessenberry's 3 Jun 2026 email ('Zycus - Next Steps... follow up to schedule a meeting') has gone unanswered for 33 days, and the buyer's own early-June circle-back window has now passed with no reply.
Buyer is not actively evaluating: 'We're not actively looking for anything. If it makes business sense, if there is a strong ROI, we will certainly do it.' (Arvind, 7 Apr 2026).

Merger resolution was expected roughly two months out (early August 2026), and Arvind's own role is uncertain ('if they decide to retain me and my function'), so the single thread carries retention risk.
Genuine AI appetite confirmed: asked if AI interest is relatively high, Arvind said 'I would say so. Yes.' and engaged with the Merlin agentic onboarding/intake pitch; AIS 6 / Moderate corroborates.
Full S2P product scope is set (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin) but no demo, ROI model, EB, or decision process has yet formed.

metrics.narrative: Pain is confirmed across three operational areas but never quantified. On 7 Apr 2026 Arvind Swamy said TEGNA's iClient CLM 'is far from perfect', supplier onboarding in Oracle 'does leave something to be desired', and spend analytics 'is all done manually' on spreadsheets, all inside a broader 'change management uphill climb' transformation. His decision lens is explicitly ROI-first: 'It depends on the cost and the ROI we can build' and 'if there is a strong ROI, we will certainly do it.' No numbers exist yet (no onboarding cycle time, FTE hours on analytics, or contract-turnaround baseline), so building a quantified value case on manual analytics and onboarding is the immediate move that unlocks a real business case.
sources: Avoma intro call 7 Apr 2026, Arvind Swamy (buyer)
Next_Step__c 3 Jun 2026 (plan to request 1-2 ROI data points)

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified. The only mapped contact is Arvind Swamy, who leads sourcing/procurement transformation but did not claim budget authority and made spend conditional on cost and ROI ('if it makes business sense... we will certainly do it'). Any commercial decision is currently frozen by the TEGNA-Nexstar merger ('we can't sign anything, we can't move anything'), and the post-merger budget structure of the combined entity is unknown ('a million dollar question... it hasn't yet been decided'). No OpportunityContactRole, MEDDPICC field, or call names a CFO or budget owner, so the evidence shows only that authority is undecided pending the merger. The move is to have Arvind name the post-merger procurement/finance budget owner once the merger resolves.
sources: Avoma intro call 7 Apr 2026, Arvind Swamy (buyer)

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's stated criteria are cost, demonstrable ROI, and sourcing-productivity gain, with an integrated-suite preference weighed against price. Arvind confirmed appetite for a single platform: 'There is benefit to do the one stop shop. It depends on the cost and the ROI we can build.' He values proven, AI-enabled capability (guided onboarding, spend analytics, Merlin/agentic) but, as an experienced buyer who has used Ariba, JAGGAER and Zycus before, wants specific feature/functionality proof rather than a broad pitch: 'I don't know that you can do a demo of everything... you can do a demo of bits and pieces.' Formal evaluation criteria (BRD, RFP, weighted scorecard) do not yet exist because the merger has paused the process.
sources: Avoma intro call 7 Apr 2026, Arvind Swamy (buyer)

decision_process.narrative: There is no defined evaluation or approval process yet; the deal is single-call, single-thread. The call sequence shows only one buyer participant (Arvind) plus a Zycus RSD and a BD associate, with no committee, procurement lead, finance, IT, or legal engaged. Arvind was explicit that process is on hold: 'we're kind of in a holding period... as time goes by and those things change... certainly we can move things forward,' and he sized the pause at 'at least two months' pending the federal-court merger hearing. The realistic near-term process is a post-merger discovery to map who owns procurement in the combined entity; that mapping is the gating move.
sources: Avoma intro call 7 Apr 2026, Arvind Swamy (buyer)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not in play and are actively blocked. Arvind stated TEGNA is in 'legal limbo' from the TEGNA-Nexstar merger (a federal-court hold in Sacramento) and 'can't sign anything.' As a new-business full-suite S2P deal, an eventual close would need an MSA, Order Form and SOW, but none has been discussed, no procurement channel or InfoSec path is known, and no contract-expiration forcing function exists (the current stack is Oracle Fusion + iClient, not a dated third-party S2P renewal). Visibility here only opens after the merger resolves and the combined-entity procurement owner is identified.
sources: Avoma intro call 7 Apr 2026, Arvind Swamy (buyer)

identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed operational pain, owned by Arvind: broken CLM ('far from perfect'), weak supplier onboarding done in Oracle ('leaves something to be desired'), fully manual spend analytics ('It's all done manually' on spreadsheets), and no intake/orchestration on indirect spend (Teams is used but procurement is punch-out via Oracle to Amazon Business/Staples). Above all, TEGNA is mid-transformation with limited resources: 'There is a lot of pain in transformation... it's a change management uphill climb... you can't change the whole thing all at once.' Inferred pain: without automation, the merger-driven consolidation and analytics burden grows. The pain is real but not yet quantified, and the pain owner (Arvind) is the same person whose post-merger role is uncertain.
sources: Avoma intro call 7 Apr 2026, Arvind Swamy (buyer)

champion.narrative: Arvind Swamy is a developing (not yet proven) champion. Positive signals: he attended Zycus' Procurement AI World event in Atlanta despite a 'tough schedule', took the 7 Apr discovery call, openly shared TEGNA's stack and pain, and expressed real AI interest. But he has shown no champion behaviour: no internal introductions (no EB, finance, IT, or legal), no shared BRD or criteria, no competitive intel volunteered, and no proactive pull to keep Zycus engaged during the freeze (the 3 Jun rep email is unanswered). His candour that he may not be retained post-merger ('if they decide to retain me and my function') makes him a single point of failure. What has developed: interest, access to himself, informed-buyer credibility. What is missing: advocacy, multi-thread, and durable authority.
sources: Avoma intro call 7 Apr 2026, Arvind Swamy (buyer)

competition.narrative: There is no live competitive evaluation; the dominant alternative is do-nothing / status quo, reinforced by the merger freeze. Arvind's references to Ariba, JAGGAER and Zycus are prior-company familiarity from NCR/Equifax/Encompass ('I have used Ariba. I have used JAGGAER... a little bit of Zycus'), not vendors TEGNA is currently evaluating, so they are dormant. The incumbents are Oracle Fusion (basic punch-out eProcurement) and iClient CLM, both acknowledged as painful. The real future threat is post-merger imposition of Nexstar's S2P stack, which could remove a selection process entirely; that is unknown until the merger resolves. Zycus' wedge is integrated-suite plus AI (Merlin agentic onboarding, iRisk supplier monitoring, Teams-embedded intake, autonomous negotiation), but it only converts if tied to quantified ROI.
sources: Avoma intro call 7 Apr 2026, Arvind Swamy (buyer)

items.text: The 7 April intro call ended with Arvind asking to circle back in two months because TEGNA can't sign anything during the Nexstar merger legal limbo, and that window has now passed unanswered.

No one is actively evaluating anyone: the real rival is the status quo (manual analytics, Oracle punch-out, broken iClient CLM), with the wildcard that the merger could impose Nexstar's procurement stack on TEGNA.
Still single-threaded to Arvind Swamy, who openly said he may not be retained post-merger, so no economic buyer, finance, IT, or legal contact has been reached.
Arvind gates any purchase on cost and provable ROI on manual analytics and onboarding, and no quantified value case exists yet to justify the price.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: An early Qualified transformation deal that has gone quiet: our only contact, Arvind, asked to pause for the TEGNA-Nexstar merger, and the circle-back window has now passed with our 3 June email unanswered, so the single move that matters is an owner-led re-engagement anchored to a quantified analytics/onboarding ROI.

win_position.text: We are early and unqualified for commitment: the only buyer thread (Arvind Swamy) is not actively evaluating ('We're not actively looking for anything'), no economic buyer is mapped, and the merger legally freezes any decision, so this reads as a low-confidence hold, not a race we are leading.

The upside is a genuine fit signal: Arvind confirmed real AI appetite ('I would say so. Yes.'), conceded the one-stop-shop has value, and named broken CLM, manual analytics and weak onboarding as live pain we directly address.
Single-thread risk is acute: everything rides on Arvind, who volunteered he may not be retained after the merger ('if they decide to retain me and my function'), so if he goes we lose the deal.
No quantified ROI yet exists, and Arvind made cost and provable ROI the gate ('It depends on the cost and the ROI we can build'), so we cannot justify the price today.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum has stalled: the last real buyer exchange was the 7 April call (90 days ago) and Marc's 3 June re-engagement email is unanswered a month later.
The buyer-set two-month circle-back window (early June) has passed without Arvind re-engaging, so the pull we were promised has not materialized.
Progress is externally gated by the TEGNA-Nexstar merger hearing, with resolution expected around early August, so no forward step is realistic until then.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is low and conditional: Arvind agreed only to 'circle back in two months' and would consider a demo of 'bits and pieces', not a full evaluation.
He did invest real time (attended the Atlanta AI event despite a tough schedule and gave a candid 30-minute discovery), signalling authentic interest even while non-committal.
No dates, budget, next meeting, or internal introductions were committed by the buyer, and the decision is explicitly frozen until the merger resolves.

deal_risk.text: Merger/political risk dominates: a federal-court hold in Sacramento freezes all TEGNA procurement decisions and could reshape the buying entity entirely.
Champion-loss risk: Arvind is the sole thread and may not survive the merger reorg, which would reset the deal to zero.

No-decision (do-nothing) risk is high: a resource-constrained transformation ('you can't change the whole thing all at once') plus an ROI-gated buyer means status quo is the most likely outcome without a compelling quantified case.

champion_strength.evidence: Developing, single-thread, self-declared retention risk; no advocacy or multi-thread (Arvind, 7 Apr 2026).

engagement.evidence: 3 Jun re-engagement email unanswered; last buyer exchange 90 days ago; circle-back window passed.

economic_buyer.evidence: No EB mapped; budget authority frozen and undecided pending merger.

scope_change.from: Full S2P suite (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin)
scope_change.to: Full S2P suite (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin)
scope_change.detail: Product scope is unchanged since creation; no modules dropped and amount steady at $110k. Arvind hinted scope could grow post-merger ('the size of the thing may be bigger') if his function is retained across the combined entity.

deal_movement.summary: The deal's pulse is cooling: one substantive buyer call on 7 Apr 2026, then rep-only outreach (9 Apr and 3 Jun emails) that has drawn no buyer reply. Ownership moved from Rishab Patel to Marc Quessenberry on 4 May 2026; stage has held at Qualified since 23 Apr 2026 with no amount or close-date change. All recent 'activity' is rep-side; the buyer has been silent for 90 days under a merger-imposed freeze.

items.change: Rep re-engagement email to Arvind to schedule post-merger discovery (unanswered)
Owner changed Rishab Patel -> Marc Quessenberry
Stage advanced Initial Interest -> Qualified; Type set to New Business
Intro discovery call with Arvind Swamy; buyer requested a two-month circle-back due to merger freeze

competitive_position.summary: There is no live competitive process. The strongest current 'competitor' is do-nothing / status quo (manual spreadsheet analytics, Oracle Fusion punch-out, iClient CLM), amplified by a resource-constrained transformation and a merger freeze that make no-decision the default. Ariba and JAGGAER are dormant, surfacing only as Arvind's prior-employer familiarity (NCR/Equifax/Encompass) on 7 Apr 2026, not as TEGNA evaluations. Oracle Fusion and iClient are the acknowledged-painful incumbents. The live unknown is whether the TEGNA-Nexstar merger imposes Nexstar's S2P stack post-merger, which could pre-empt any selection; that must be uncovered in post-merger discovery (expected around August 2026).

competitors.name: Do-nothing / status quo (manual analytics + Oracle punch-out)
competitors.quote: Do you do analytics? It's all done manually.
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 7 Apr 2026, Arvind Swamy
competitors.how_we_win: Convert the pain into a quantified ROI on manual-analytics hours and onboarding cycle time; a resource-constrained team ('you can't change the whole thing all at once') only moves when the value math is undeniable.

competitors.name: Oracle Fusion ERP (incumbent eProcurement)
competitors.quote: We have Orca Fusion for ERP... We do our Punch Out Checkout through the Fusion app.
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 7 Apr 2026, Arvind Swamy
competitors.how_we_win: Fusion is punch-out only with no intake/orchestration or AI; layer Merlin intake in Teams (Arvind confirmed Teams usage) on top of Oracle via API rather than ripping out the ERP.

competitors.name: iClient CLM (incumbent)
competitors.quote: You know, yeah, there are issues... it's far from perfect.
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 7 Apr 2026, Arvind Swamy
competitors.how_we_win: iClient is acknowledged broken; position iContract with Merlin contract authoring, risk flagging and renewal alerts as part of the integrated suite, no integration tax.

competitors.name: Post-merger Nexstar S2P stack (potential imposition)
competitors.quote: A million dollar question. Right? We just started it two weeks ago. It hasn't yet been decided.
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 7 Apr 2026, Arvind Swamy
competitors.how_we_win: Get ahead of the combined-entity decision: in post-merger discovery, map whether Nexstar has an incumbent S2P and secure Arvind (or his successor) as the internal voice for a modern AI-first standard before a stack is imposed.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.quote: I have used Ariba. I have used JAGGAER, etcetera, and a little bit of Zycus. Over time. So I understand the scope and what these tools can do for us.
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 7 Apr 2026, Arvind Swamy (prior-employer familiarity, not a TEGNA evaluation)
competitors.how_we_win: If it surfaces in a real eval, lead with Merlin agentic negotiation and iRisk real-time monitoring versus JAGGAER's modular, phased complexity.

competitors.name: Ariba (SAP)
competitors.quote: I have used Ariba. I have used JAGGAER, etcetera, and a little bit of Zycus.
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 7 Apr 2026, Arvind Swamy (prior-employer familiarity, not a TEGNA evaluation)
competitors.how_we_win: If it surfaces, contrast Merlin orchestration embedded in Teams and an AI-first roadmap against SAP's legacy S2P and slower innovation cadence.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit is directionally strong on capability and AI but entirely gated by cost/ROI and the merger freeze. Arvind values an integrated suite and AI automation but will only move on proven value math, and has committed to nothing beyond a two-month pause.

best_practice_check.summary: This is an early, single-thread deal now stalled behind a merger freeze; the win path is an owner-led restart, a quantified ROI hook, and a second thread that survives the reorg.
flags: Restart the thread now: Marc should re-contact Arvind directly because the buyer's early-June circle-back window has passed and the 3 June email is unanswered a month later.
Break single-thread dependency: Arvind may not be retained post-merger, so identify and reach a second procurement/finance stakeholder in the combined entity before the reorg.
Lead with a quantified analytics + onboarding ROI, not a generic full-suite demo; Arvind explicitly gates any move on cost and provable value.

items.name: Arvind Swamy
items.title: Sourcing & procurement transformation lead (self-described on 7 Apr call; no Salesforce title on file)
items.risk: Sole thread and a developing champion; openly uncertain he will be retained post-merger ('if they decide to retain me and my function'), so a reorg could reset the deal. Has not multi-threaded or named a budget owner.
items.source: OpportunityContactRole / task contact; Avoma intro call 7 Apr 2026

champion_strength.summary: Arvind Swamy is a developing, single-thread champion: real interest (attended the Atlanta AI event, candid discovery, confirmed AI appetite and integrated-suite value) but no advocacy yet, no internal introductions, no shared criteria, and an unanswered 3 June follow-up. His candidly uncertain post-merger retention makes him a single point of failure. What has developed: interest and access to himself. What is missing: advocacy, multi-thread, and durable authority.
champion_strength.source: Avoma intro call 7 Apr 2026; Next_Step__c 3 Jun 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The full Merlin/agentic story (guided onboarding, iRisk, Teams-embedded intake, autonomous negotiation, spend analytics) was delivered at length on 7 April and landed as awareness, not desire, because the buyer was passive ('we're not actively looking') and it was not tied to the quantified analytics/onboarding ROI Arvind requires. Re-anchor the agentic narrative to his specific pain and value math to convert interest into pull.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 6 / Moderate is corroborated by the call: TEGNA already runs AI apps for AP/invoicing and Arvind confirmed AI interest is 'relatively high' ('I would say so. Yes.'), but appetite is gated by ROI and the merger, so it reads as curious rather than hungry.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal is cooling: last genuine buyer exchange was 90 days ago (7 Apr), the buyer's own early-June circle-back window has passed, and the 3 June rep email is unanswered. The pause is buyer-imposed and time-bounded (merger), so this is a monitored slow-down, not yet a dead deal, but re-engagement is overdue.

All post-7-April activity is rep-side; no buyer pull since the intro call.
